title: Repeating group (paneldynamic)
description: paneldynamic — a repeating group: the respondent fills the same set of template elements once per item they add (a household member, a device, a prior address). The answer is an ARRAY of objects, one per occurrence, e.g. [{"kind": "laptop", "nickname": "work"}, {"kind": "phone", "nickname": "personal"}]. The warehouse decomposes a panel into one sub-question per template element (stable_name "devices.kind", "devices.nickname", …) and the array POSITION becomes the fact grain's occurrence — so the same person's two devices are two rows at occurrence 1 and 2, never conflated. A panel cell keeps its own type: a single-select cell (radiogroup/dropdown) resolves to an option_key; a free-text cell (text/comment) flows to value_text under its own pii_risk, copied to the PII store and screened per occurrence. Only those cell types are supported — the publish gate rejects multi-select / scalar / matrix / nested-panel cells so a panel answer can't be silently dropped.

elements.name: devices
elements.title: List the devices you use for work
elements.description: templateElements are the per-occurrence sub-questions. Occurrences are not fixed in the definition — the respondent adds as many as they need — so the warehouse derives occurrence from the answer array position, not from the definition.

templateElements.name: nickname
templateElements.title: What do you call it? (free text)
templateElements.pii_risk_rationale: a device nickname can name its owner; screened per occurrence
